findings.claim: Under TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=TRUE TESTTHAT_CPUS=4 the full suite showed FAIL 2 in test-shared-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R with a blank `actual` value (the renderUI shared picker read before binding — a boot-tail race), but a SERIAL re-run of that file passed FAIL 0 / PASS 3, confirming it is a parallel-load flake and not a product failure.
findings.detail: Concretely validated CLAUDE.md's "residual flake under parallel load is not a product failure — re-confirm serially" rule; the file uses only string formulas (no expr()), so the normalize change cannot affect it. Full suite otherwise PASS 3463 / SKIP 0.
findings.verification: Read tests/testthat/test-shared-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R @HEAD: it uses string formulas only (0 expr()/quote()/rlang::expr matches), so a normalize/capture change cannot affect it — confirming the finding's 'no expr()' detail. The renderUI shared-picker assertions live around l40-49 (get_html('#shared_grp') then grepl on actual HTML), consistent with the cited boot-tail-race symptom (blank `actual`). set_sentinel hardening for boot-tail contention is present in tests/testthat/helper-super-pressure.R (l127-147, explicit 'contention that leaves the very first set_sentinel reading an empty DOM' guard). Per orientation this flake class is CONFIRMED current this session (green serially). The specific FAIL 2 + PASS 3463 counts are a point-in-time run observation, not re-runnable here, but the structural claim and the parallel-load-flake characterization hold. Cited lines 41/46 drifted slightly (assertions now ~42/48) but same content.

findings.claim: A built (eager) ggplot object deparses to `<object>` and cannot be translated, so it must be rejected; the discriminator is.call(x) || rlang::is_symbol(x) cleanly accepts language objects (rlang::expr()/quote()) and rejects built ggplot objects — implemented in R/paintr-shared-ui.R normalize_shared_formulas (l141, with the rationale comment l123-130) and mirrored in ptr_capture_formula's accept() local (R/paintr-app.R l223-224: rlang::is_string(x) || is.call(x) || rlang::is_symbol(x)).
findings.detail: This is why the ptr_shared formulas contract requires `expr()`/`quote()`-quoted language objects, not eager ggplot objects. The discriminator `is.call(x) || rlang::is_symbol(x)` cleanly separates acceptable language objects (TRUE) from built ggplot objects (both FALSE, is.language also FALSE).
findings.verification: Read R/paintr-shared-ui.R @HEAD: normalize_shared_formulas (l130) gates on `is.call(x) || rlang::is_symbol(x)` (l141), preceded by the comment block l123-130 stating a built ggplot object 'deparses to <object> and cannot be translated' and that the fn only deparses language objects via expr_text width. R/paintr-app.R ptr_capture_formula accept() (l223-224) = rlang::is_string(x) || is.call(x) || rlang::is_symbol(x). The finding's primary source `R/paintr-builtins.R:357-391` is now STALE — that region @HEAD is ptr_builtin_expr_build_ui (seed-textarea), unrelated to the discriminator; the load-bearing code is in shared-ui.R/app.R as in the finding's own checked_refs. Core claim (built ggplot undeparseable -> reject; is.call||is_symbol discriminator) holds; new_claim re-points source to the live locations.

findings.claim: ptr_capture_formula() is a front-door normalizer that turns any captured argument (string / symbol / expr-quo-quote-bquote wrapper / string-builder call / ggplot expression) into a single STRING via rlang::expr_text(width=500L), so the entire ggpaintr engine downstream of the public boundary is string-only and expression mode is purely a deparse convenience.
findings.verification: R/paintr-app.R `ptr_capture_formula` (def at line 196) confirmed: unwraps {} blocks, returns strings as-is, resolves symbols via envir+stack-walk, unwraps expr/quo/quote/bquote, force-evals paste/paste0/sprintf/c/[[/$ string-builder calls, and the terminal path at line 290 `return(rlang::expr_text(formula_captured, width = 500L))` deparses any remaining call/symbol to a single string. It is the front-door normalizer (called at app.R:141, 162, 1094 for ptr_app/ptr_ui/ptr_server). Claim holds exactly.

findings.claim: Expression-mode formula input now exists at the single-formula entries (ptr_app/ptr_server/ptr_ui via ptr_capture_formula) AND at ptr_shared (via normalize_shared_formulas, shared-coordinator.R:263); only ptr_app_grid remains string-only, still guarded by all(vapply(plots, rlang::is_string, logical(1))) at R/paintr-app.R:1322. (ADR 0009 §28 originally scoped expr mode to single-formula entries; ptr_shared was extended afterward.)
findings.detail: ptr_app_grid is unmaintained, so this session extended expression mode to ptr_shared only; ptr_app_grid's identical guard at R/paintr-app.R:1318-1322 was intentionally left string-only.
findings.verification: Confirmed three facts: (1) single-formula entries use ptr_capture_formula (app.R:141/162/1094). (2) ptr_shared accepts expressions via normalize_shared_formulas, called at R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:263 (exact line in claim) and defined at R/paintr-shared-ui.R:128. (3) ptr_app_grid remains string-only: R/paintr-app.R:1327 `all(vapply(plots, rlang::is_string, logical(1)))` with an explicit abort ('ptr_app_grid() requires every plot to be a string; expression mode is not supported here'). Line drifted from 1322 to ~1324-1327 but symbol/guard intact. Claim holds.

findings.claim: R's native pipe `|>` is syntax desugared by the parser at parse time, so `rlang::expr(mtcars |> ggplot(aes(...)))` captures the already-rewritten `ggplot(mtcars, aes(...))` (the `|>` surface is gone), whereas `%>%` is a real operator preserved verbatim and string mode preserves `|>` via preprocess_native_pipe(); the plot is identical, only the displayed code surface differs.
findings.detail: This caveat is inherited by ptr_shared from the single-formula entries; it is a documented limitation, not a behavioral guarantee, so it intentionally has no test.
findings.verification: R/paintr-translate.R confirms `preprocess_native_pipe` is defined at line 522 and applied to the formula string at line 41 (`preprocessed <- preprocess_native_pipe(formula)`), so string mode rewrites/preserves `|>`; a comment at line 33-34 states explicitly 'In expression mode `|>` is already desugared at parse time, so only `%>%` can win'. The expr-capture behavioral claim (R desugars `|>` at parse time so rlang::expr captures the rewritten call, while `%>%` is a real operator preserved verbatim) is a property of R's parser and is documented verbatim in the source comment. Claim holds.

findings.claim: ptr_shared(formulas) now accepts each element as either a string or a quoted ggplot expression (language object), implemented by a new internal normalize_shared_formulas() that deparses language elements (expr_text width=500L), keeps strings byte-identical, and rejects anything else with an index-naming error; the signature is UNCHANGED (formulas=, normally evaluated — no enexpr/!!!/envir) because expr()/quote() already evaluate to recoverable language objects.
findings.detail: ptr_shared() normalizes once at the top (before shared_translate_formulas) so spec$formulas and shared_assert_panel_consumer_sources both see strings; shared_translate_formulas re-normalizes idempotently. The string path is identity through the normalizer (string -> return(x)), so all existing all-string call sites are byte-for-byte unaffected. Additive + trivially reversible -> no ADR was warranted (failed the "hard to reverse" criterion).
findings.verification: Re-verified at HEAD a1737f7. The sole intervening diff to the cited file(s) since this finding's baseline is b6a2f72 (the behavior-preserving partition_view seam, ADR 0026 #11), read in full: it only replaced three host_owned_keys derivations + one render_shared_section literal with equivalent partition_view accessor calls and added R/paintr-partition-view.R. It does not intersect this claim's code path; cited symbols and behavior confirmed unchanged. (Line numbers in R/paintr-app.R after ~1168 shifted +4 and in R/paintr-shared-ui.R after ~416 shifted +3; verify by symbol.)
